name: CRUD/conditional-update
description: Conditional update (PUT /Patient?search-params, without a resource id in the URL) conformance tests: no match upserts (creates), no criteria is rejected, a client-supplied id with no match creates that id, one match updates the resolved resource, a body id matching/mismatching the resolved resource id is accepted/rejected, and multiple matches are rejected with 412. Uses Patient fixtures tagged with unique identifiers (http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update) to avoid collisions with other suites; client-supplied ids are used where the test needs to deterministically match or mismatch a resource id, since request bodies are static fixtures.

variable.name: seedOneMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient unconditionally created in setup that carries identifier CU-ONEMATCH

operation.description: Unconditionally create the Patient that later conditional-update attempts should match exactly once (CU-ONEMATCH)
PUT a client-id Patient (CU-CORRECTID) so later tests can deterministically match or mismatch its id in a conditional update body
Unconditionally create the first of two Patients sharing identifier CU-MULTI
Unconditionally create the second of two Patients sharing identifier CU-MULTI

test.name: Conditional update with no existing match upserts a new resource
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-NOMATCH with a body that has no id. No resource matches, so the server must upsert (create) it and return 201 Created.

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
Server must have assigned an id

test.name: Conditional update with no search criteria is rejected
test.description: PUT /Patient with no query string at all. A conditional update requires selective search criteria; with none, the server must return 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient with no query string

test.name: Conditional update with a client-supplied id and no existing match creates that id
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-EXPLICIT with a body that has a client-supplied id. No resource matches, so the server must create a new resource using the client-supplied id and return 201 Created.

assert.description: Server must have used the client-supplied id

test.name: Conditional update with no id in body and one match updates the existing resource
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-ONEMATCH with a body that has no id. Exactly one Patient already carries that identifier (created in setup), so the server must update it in place, returning 200 OK with the same server-assigned id.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-ONEMATCH, matches the single seeded Patient

assert.description: Must return HTTP 200 OK (updated, not created)
Returned id must match the existing seeded resource's id

test.name: Conditional update with a body id matching the resolved resource updates its content
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-CORRECTID with a body whose id equals the id of the single matched resource (both are the client-supplied id fhir262-cond-upd-correctid from setup). The ids agree, so the server must accept the update and return 200 OK with the new content (gender changed to female).

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-CORRECTID, body id matches the resolved resource's id

assert.description: Content must reflect the update (gender changed to female)

test.name: Conditional update with a mismatched body id is rejected
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-CORRECTID with a body whose id (fhir262-cond-upd-wrongid) disagrees with the id of the single resource the search criteria resolves to (fhir262-cond-upd-correctid). The server must reject this with 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-CORRECTID, body id deliberately disagrees with the resolved resource's id

test.name: Conditional update with multiple existing matches fails
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-update|CU-MULTI. Two Patients already carry that identifier (created in setup), so the criteria is not selective enough to identify a single resource; the server must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-MULTI, matches both seeded Patients
Teardown: conditional delete the CU-NOMATCH Patient
Teardown: conditional delete the CU-EXPLICIT Patient
Teardown: conditional delete the CU-ONEMATCH Patient
Teardown: conditional delete the CU-CORRECTID Patient
Teardown: conditional delete both CU-MULTI Patients
